tasks.description: Preserve the existing shot-group click-selection path while touching timeline-agent plumbing: keep `TimelineCanvas` label clicks calling `onSelectClips(group.clipIds)`, keep `TimelineEditor` wiring that prop to `selectClips`, and do not introduce a redundant shot-group selection callback or second selection path.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the existing shot-group selection contract is still the only path: `TimelineCanvas` label single-click calls `onSelectClips(group.clipIds)` and `TimelineEditor` still wires `onSelectClips={selectClips}` with no redundant shot-group selection callback present. Confirmed with the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Current `TimelineCanvas`/`TimelineEditor` code preserves the original shot-group single-click selection contract and does not add a redundant selection callback.

tasks.description: Extend the shared selected-clip contract with optional shot metadata on both sides of the boundary. Update frontend persisted attachment types in `src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts` and backend payload types in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/types.ts` so `shotId`/`shotName` and `shot_id`/`shot_name` are accepted without breaking legacy sessions, gallery attachments, or older calls.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended the shared selected-clip type boundary only: frontend persisted attachments now accept optional `shotId`/`shotName`, and backend selected-clip payloads now accept optional `shot_id`/`shot_name`. The additions are optional, so legacy session turns, gallery-only attachments, and older request bodies remain valid. Verified with the full `useAgentSession.test.tsx` and `selectedClips.test.ts` modules.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The shared selected-clip boundary was extended additively with optional shot metadata on both frontend and backend types.

tasks.description: Plumb shot metadata through the frontend send path. Update `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts` to resolve shot membership from `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` plus `useShots()` data, preserve `shotId`/`shotName` when `AgentChat` merges timeline and gallery selections, and include `shot_id`/`shot_name` in `useSendMessage()` request bodies. Do not widen `ResolvedTimelineConfig` just to carry pinned groups unless execution uncovers a separate hard need.
tasks.executor_notes: Frontend shot metadata now resolves from `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` plus `useShots()`, not `resolvedConfig`. `AgentChat` preserves `shotId`/`shotName` when timeline and gallery selections share a URL, and `useSendMessage()` now includes optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` fields in `selected_clips` without changing legacy attachment behavior. Verified with the full `AgentChat.test.tsx` and `useAgentSession.test.tsx` modules.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Frontend shot resolution uses `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`, merged attachments retain shot metadata, and the send payload includes `shot_id`/`shot_name`.

tasks.description: Preserve shot metadata through backend normalization, persistence, and continuation recovery. Update `normalizeSelectedClips()`, `attachSelectedClips()`, and `recoverSelectedClipsFromTurns()` so explicit shot fields survive request parsing, persisted turn attachments, and follow-up turns. Keep prompt enrichment behavior intact and never overwrite explicit shot metadata supplied by the frontend.
tasks.executor_notes: Backend selected-clip normalization, persisted user-turn attachments, and continuation recovery now round-trip optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` and `shotId`/`shotName` fields. The changes are additive-only, and prompt enrichment still prefers explicit prompt metadata before DB-derived prompts, so explicit client-supplied shot fields are preserved untouched. Verified with the full `selectedClips.test.ts` and `loop.test.ts` modules.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Backend normalization, attachment persistence, and continuation recovery now round-trip shot metadata without overwriting explicit client values.

tasks.description: Add explicit shot-name loading and structured timeline summary output on the backend. Extend `loadTimelineState()` to batch-load shot names for pinned shot IDs from `shots`, thread `shotNamesById` through `TimelineState`, `ToolContext`, and the `view_timeline` path, and update timeline formatting so clip lines include shot context and the summary includes a `Shot groups:` section with resolved `shotName` or `shotId`, `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`.
tasks.executor_notes: Timeline state now batch-loads shot names from `shots` for pinned shot ids, threads them through `TimelineState`, `ToolContext`, and the `view_timeline` path, and uses the same shot-aware summary for both loop-time prompt construction and runtime `run(command="view")`. Clip lines now include shot context, and summaries include a `Shot groups:` section with resolved names or `shotId` fallback plus `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`. Verified with the full `loop.test.ts` and `tools/timeline.test.ts` modules.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Timeline state now loads shot names and both loop-time summaries and `run(command="view")` share the same shot-aware timeline formatter.

tasks.description: Centralize same-shot semantics in one backend helper and apply it everywhere that depends on shot context. Add a shared resolver in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.ts` that prefers explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership for timeline clips, then `findShotForGenerations()` as fallback; use it from `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()`. Keep `duplicate_generation` on the fast path when selected clips already share a `shot_id`, and update prompt/system-prompt text so shared-shot context is surfaced once structured shot data is available.
tasks.executor_notes: Added one shared resolver in `tools/clips.ts` that resolves shot context in priority order: explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership for selected timeline clips, then `findShotForGenerations()` fallback. `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()` now all use that helper, and `duplicate_generation` keeps the fast path because explicit shared `shot_id` returns before any generation-history lookup. Prompt text now surfaces structured selected-clip shot metadata and shared-shot context to the model. Verified with the full `tools/clips.test.ts`, `tools/create-task.test.ts`, and `loop.test.ts` modules.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Same-shot semantics are centralized in `resolveSelectedClipShot()` and used by loop defaults, task creation, LoRA updates, and duplicate-generation.

tasks.description: Update existing automated suites only; do not add new test files. Extend current edge and frontend tests to cover: shot metadata normalization and recovery, selected-clip prompt shot context, timeline summary shot-group output, same-shot helper behavior and duplicate fast path, frontend attachment preservation/send payloads, and the existing `TimelineCanvas` shot-group single-click selection regression. Prefer adding cases to existing suites such as `selectedClips.test.ts`, `loop.test.ts`, `tools/clips.test.ts`, `tools/create-task.test.ts`, `tools/timeline.test.ts`, `AgentChat.test.tsx`, `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`.
tasks.executor_notes: Extended existing edge and frontend suites only. Added coverage for selected-clip shot metadata normalization, continuation recovery round-trip, selected-clip prompt shot context, shared-shot system prompt guidance, timeline summary shot-group output, same-shot resolver precedence, duplicate fast-path behavior when selected clips already carry a shared explicit `shot_id`, create-task shot reuse, and frontend attachment merge/send payload preservation. The existing `TimelineCanvas` single-click shot-group selection regression remains covered by the unchanged full module. Verified with the full edge and frontend Vitest modules.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Coverage was added by extending existing suites in place, including timeline summaries, round-trip metadata, prompt/system-prompt shot context, duplicate fast path, and frontend send-path preservation.

tasks.description: Run verification to completion and fix any failures before re-running. Execute focused edge tests with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.edge.config.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/selectedClips.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/loop.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/create-task.test.ts supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/timeline.test.ts`, execute focused frontend tests with `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.test.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx`, and, before finishing, write a short throwaway script that reproduces the shot-context bug end to end, run it to confirm the fix, then delete the script.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran the full focused edge and frontend Vitest modules to completion and confirmed they pass with the batch changes applied. Wrote and ran a short temporary reproduction script under the repo's edge Vitest config to verify the shot-context pipeline end to end: request normalization preserved shot metadata, persisted attachments carried shotId/shotName, continuation recovery restored them, prompt/system-prompt text surfaced shared shot context, and same-shot resolution stayed on the explicit-shot fast path without a fallback lookup. The temporary reproduction script was then deleted.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Verification is complete for the automated scope: I re-ran the full edge suite and the targeted frontend suites successfully. The manual smoke criterion remains informational and waived.

watch_items: Use `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` for frontend shot membership resolution; `ResolvedTimelineConfig` does not expose `pinnedShotGroups`.
Keep the existing `TimelineCanvas` -> `onSelectClips(group.clipIds)` -> `TimelineEditor.selectClips` path unchanged; that behavior already exists and already has regression coverage.
Shot metadata must stay optional end to end so older sessions, stored turns, and gallery-only attachments remain valid.
Gallery selections may legitimately have `generationId` without any shot metadata; do not invent fake shot names on the frontend.
Do not overwrite explicit `shot_id` or `shot_name` from the client when enriching clips from generation metadata.
Backend timeline summaries cannot derive shot names from `pinnedShotGroups` alone; batch-load names from `shots` and fall back to raw `shotId` when needed.
One shared resolver should define same-shot semantics for loop defaults, active reference lookup, task shot reuse, travel LoRAs, and duplicate behavior.
`executeDuplicateGeneration()` should skip `findShotForGenerations()` when selected clips already provide a single shared explicit `shot_id`.
Do not broaden this work into unrelated AgentChat rendering or undo/realtime behavior changes; existing debt in those areas should not get worse.
Do not add new test files; extend existing suites and keep new or modified files reasonably small.
If runtime `run(command="view")` and loop-time summaries diverge, treat that as a bug; both paths must use the same shot-name data.
Manual smoke is useful after tests: select a pinned shot group, send an agent message, continue the session, and confirm shot context survives the continuation turn.

sense_checks.question: Did the timeline UI keep the existing shot-group single-click selection contract intact, with no redundant `onShotGroupSelect`-style path added?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The current UI still uses the original single-click path `TimelineCanvas -> onSelectClips(group.clipIds) -> TimelineEditor.selectClips`, and repository search found no added `onShotGroupSelect`-style callback or second selection path.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by current TimelineCanvas/TimelineEditor code and the passing TimelineCanvas suite.

sense_checks.question: Do frontend attachments and backend selected-clip payloads both accept optional shot id/name fields without breaking legacy call sites or stored data?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Frontend persisted attachments accept optional `shotId`/`shotName`, backend selected-clip payloads accept optional `shot_id`/`shot_name`, and both changes are additive-only so existing attachment/session shapes still pass unchanged.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by additive type changes in `agent-session.ts` and `types.ts`, plus passing serialization/send tests.

sense_checks.question: Does the frontend resolve shot metadata from `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`, preserve it through `AgentChat`, and send `shot_id` and `shot_name` in `selected_clips` for timeline-selected media?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. Timeline-selected media now resolves shot membership from `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`, `AgentChat` preserves timeline shot metadata when deduping against gallery selections, and `useSendMessage()` serializes optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` fields in `selected_clips`.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by `useSelectedMediaClips()`, `AgentChat`, and `useSendMessage()` code paths and passing frontend tests.

sense_checks.question: Do normalization, persisted attachments, and continuation recovery round-trip shot metadata without clobbering explicit client-supplied values?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `normalizeSelectedClips()`, `attachSelectedClips()`, and `recoverSelectedClipsFromTurns()` now round-trip optional shot metadata, and prompt enrichment still only backfills prompt text when the client did not already provide it, so explicit shot fields are not clobbered.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by `selectedClips.ts` and `loop.ts` round-trip logic and passing edge tests.

sense_checks.question: Does timeline state now load shot names from `shots`, and does the timeline summary include both clip-level shot annotations and a `Shot groups:` section with resolved names or `shotId` fallback?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `loadTimelineState()` now loads shot names from `shots`, and the shared `viewTimeline()` summary adds shot annotations to clip lines plus a `Shot groups:` section with resolved shot label or `shotId` fallback, `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by `db.ts`, `registry.ts`, and `timeline.ts`, plus timeline summary test coverage.

sense_checks.question: Do `runAgentLoop`, `executeCreateTask`, `executeSetLora`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration` all rely on one shared shot resolver that prefers explicit shot metadata before fallback inference?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()` now all resolve shot context through the shared `resolveSelectedClipShot()` helper, which prefers explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-shot-group membership, then generation-history fallback.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by the shared resolver implementation and its use in loop, create-task, set-lora, and duplicate-generation paths.

sense_checks.question: Were the required coverage gaps closed by extending existing test suites only, including timeline summaries, prompt/roundtrip behavior, duplicate fast path, send payloads, and the existing shot-group click-selection regression?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The required coverage gaps are closed by extending existing suites only: selected-clip shot metadata normalization and continuation recovery in `selectedClips.test.ts` and `loop.test.ts`, prompt and shared-shot system-prompt context in `loop.test.ts`, timeline summary shot-group output in `tools/timeline.test.ts`, same-shot resolver precedence and duplicate fast path in `tools/clips.test.ts`, create-task shot reuse in `tools/create-task.test.ts`, frontend merge/send payload preservation in `AgentChat.test.tsx` and `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, and the existing shot-group single-click selection regression remains covered by the full `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` module run.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed by updated existing suites only; no new scoped test files were needed.

sense_checks.question: Did the targeted Vitest runs pass after any needed fixes, and was the temporary reproduction script run successfully and then removed?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The targeted edge and frontend Vitest runs passed, and the temporary shot-context reproduction script was run successfully under the edge Vitest config and then removed.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed for the automated scope by rerunning the full edge suite and targeted frontend suites successfully.

meta_commentary: Treat this as one shared shot-context pipeline, not a set of isolated patches. The only accepted correction to the approved plan is the frontend source-path fix already called out by gate review: `useSelectedMediaClips()` must read pinned groups from editor `data.config`, not `resolvedConfig`. Keep the existing shot-group click UX untouched, avoid unrelated refactors in AgentChat or timeline persistence, and make the backend shot resolver the single source of truth so summaries, prompts, travel defaults, LoRA updates, and duplicate semantics cannot drift apart.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Preserve the existing shot-group selection path instead of adding new canvas selection plumbing.
Extend shared selected-clip and attachment types with optional shot metadata.
Enrich frontend-selected clips with shot context and send that metadata to the agent.
Preserve shot metadata through backend normalization, attachment persistence, and continuation recovery.
Load explicit shot names on the backend and include structured shot context in timeline summaries.
Centralize same-shot resolution across loop, task creation, LoRA updates, duplicate behavior, and prompt guidance.
Add focused backend and frontend coverage for shot summaries, roundtrip metadata, prompt context, duplicate fast path, and send-path behavior.

validation.completeness_notes: All approved plan steps are mapped. Execution guidance incorporates the gate-approved correction for step 3 by sourcing pinned groups from `useTimelineEditorData().data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`. Step 7 is satisfied by extending existing test suites rather than adding new test files, because this finalize brief must honor the explicit execution constraint against creating new test files.
